title: CLI Argument Contract Sweep
findings: 已读 registry 并排除 ISS-20260609-021..042、ISS-20260531-114、ISS-20260608-031/034 等同类旧项；扫描 packages/cli 命令定义、shared i18n、README/docs 和 CLI surface snapshots。新增 2 个 distinct CLI 参数契约问题：install global 分支绕过 dry-run；sync continue/abort 互斥缺失。
issues_found: [{"title":"fabric install --global ignores the declared --dry-run no-write contract","severity":"medium","description":"install-v2 declares --dry-run as printing the install plan without writing files, but runInitCommand checks args.global first and immediately calls runGlobalInstall({url}) without passing or honoring dry-run. runGlobalInstall then runs installGlobalCore, writes global ~/.fabric state, and mounts/clones a URL when provided. A user running fabric install --global --dry-run or --global --url ... --dry-run gets real global writes despite the shared dry-run flag contract.","location":"packages/cli/src/commands/install-v2.ts:38; packages/cli/src/commands/install-v2.ts:87; packages/cli/src/install/run-global-install.ts:117; packages/cli/src/install/run-global-install.ts:135","fix_direction":"Either reject --dry-run combined with --global as an explicit invalid combination, or extend runGlobalInstall with a planOnly/dryRun mode that reports global config/store clone/mount actions without writing. Add a CLI-level regression for fabric install --global --dry-run asserting no ~/.fabric writes and no git clone.","affected_components":["fabric install","install-v2","global install","dry-run contract"],"tags":["cli-arg-contracts","dry-run","flag-contradiction","no-write-contract"]},{"title":"fabric sync accepts --continue and --abort together and silently prioritizes continue","severity":"medium","description":"sync exposes two mutually exclusive resume actions, --continue and --abort, but the command handler only checks args.continue first and returns before inspecting args.abort. If both flags are supplied, the abort request is ignored and runContinueSync executes. This contradicts the documented conflict-resolution model where continue and abort are alternatives, and can run the more state-advancing path when the user also requested abort.","location":"packages/cli/src/commands/sync.ts:29; packages/cli/src/commands/sync.ts:35","fix_direction":"Add an explicit pre-dispatch guard: if args.continue && args.abort, print an actionable usage error and set a nonzero exit before calling either resume path. Cover the command-level flag combination in tests, not only the lower-level state-machine helpers.","affected_components":["fabric sync","sync command parser","conflict resume flow"],"tags":["cli-arg-contracts","mutually-exclusive-flags","sync","flag-contradiction"]}]
